redFlags.title: Director Files ITR-1 Instead of ITR-2
redFlags.description: A person who is a director in a private or public limited company at any time during FY 2025-26 is ineligible for ITR-1 and must file ITR-2. This applies even if the only income is salary and no director fee is received.
redFlags.consequence: Return treated as defective u/s 139(9). Notice issued with 15-day window to rectify. If not rectified, return is invalid and treated as not filed, leading to penalties u/s 271F and interest u/s 234A.
redFlags.correction: File a revised return using ITR-2 before the due date. After due date, file revised return (if original filed on time) or belated return. Disclose directorship details in the Director Disclosure section.

redFlags.title: Using Pre-23-July-2024 Capital Gains Rates for Post-July Transactions
redFlags.description: Finance Act 2024 changed capital gains rates with effect from 23 July 2024. STCG u/s 111A increased from 15% to 20%. LTCG u/s 112A rate increased from 10% to 12.5% but exemption limit raised from ₹1 lakh to ₹1.25 lakh. Transactions must be split by this date.
redFlags.consequence: Underreporting of tax leads to interest u/s 234A/234B/234C and penalty u/s 270A for misreporting (50% to 200% of tax payable on misreported income).
redFlags.correction: Segregate all capital gains transactions into two periods: 1 April 2025 – 22 July 2025 (old rates: 15% STCG, 10% LTCG) and 23 July 2025 – 31 March 2026 (new rates: 20% STCG, 12.5% LTCG). The ITR-2 form has separate sub-rows for this.

redFlags.title: Claiming 87A Rebate on Special Rate Income (STCG 111A / LTCG 112A)
redFlags.description: Section 87A rebate (₹60,000 for income up to ₹12 lakh under new regime) is NOT available against tax payable on STCG u/s 111A and LTCG u/s 112A. Many taxpayers incorrectly offset the rebate against their entire tax including special rate taxes.
redFlags.consequence: Underpayment of tax. The income tax portal now restricts this computation automatically, but manual errors can lead to short-tax payment and subsequent notice u/s 156.
redFlags.correction: Apply 87A rebate only against tax on normal income (slab income). Separately compute 20% on STCG 111A and 12.5% on LTCG 112A. These amounts are not reduced by 87A rebate.

redFlags.title: Resident (ROR) Not Reporting Foreign Assets in Schedule FA
redFlags.description: Every Resident and Ordinarily Resident (ROR) individual who holds any foreign asset (bank account, shares, property, beneficial interest) must disclose it in Schedule FA. Omission constitutes a violation of the Black Money (Undisclosed Foreign Income and Assets) and Imposition of Tax Act, 2015.
redFlags.consequence: Penalty of ₹10 lakh per undisclosed foreign asset. Additional prosecution risk with imprisonment of 3 to 10 years. The undisclosed foreign asset is taxed at flat 30% with no deduction or exemption.
redFlags.correction: File revised return to include Schedule FA with full details of all foreign accounts, assets, and income. If return has already been assessed, file under Vivad Se Vishwas or approach the AO proactively.

redFlags.title: NRI Claiming Section 87A Rebate
redFlags.description: Section 87A rebate is available ONLY to individuals who are resident in India. NRIs and RNOR taxpayers cannot claim this rebate irrespective of their total income level.
redFlags.consequence: Incorrect rebate of up to ₹60,000 claimed. Demand notice u/s 143(1) after processing for short payment. Interest u/s 234B/234C on shortfall.
redFlags.correction: Do not claim 87A rebate when residency status is NRI. Compute full tax on income and adjust only TDS deducted at source for the refund/payment calculation.

redFlags.title: Carrying Forward Capital Losses Without Filing Return on Time
redFlags.description: Capital losses can be carried forward to subsequent years ONLY if the return of income for the year of loss is filed on or before the due date u/s 139(1). A belated return filed after 31 July 2026 (for AY 2026-27) cannot carry forward any capital losses.
redFlags.consequence: Capital losses permanently lapse and cannot be utilized in future years. This is especially costly for large loss positions from equity market downturns.
redFlags.correction: Always file return on or before the due date if capital losses exist. If belated return is already filed, losses from that year cannot be reinstated.

redFlags.category: House Property – New Regime Deduction Error
redFlags.title: Claiming Home Loan Interest Deduction for Self-Occupied Property under New Regime
redFlags.description: Under the new tax regime (Section 115BAC), the deduction u/s 24(b) for interest on home loan for self-occupied property is NOT available. Only let-out property interest is allowed as it reduces the income from that property (but overall net loss cannot be set off).
redFlags.consequence: Inflated deductions reduce taxable income incorrectly. On scrutiny, deduction disallowed, tax computed on higher income, leading to demand, interest, and penalty.
redFlags.correction: Under new regime: self-occupied property interest deduction = NIL. For let-out property, 30% standard deduction and actual interest can be deducted from rental income. If net loss from HP exists, it cannot be set off against other income under new regime.

redFlags.title: Dividend and Interest Income Not Matching AIS
redFlags.description: CBDT's Annual Information Statement (AIS) captures dividend paid by all companies, interest paid by banks, and MF dividends. If the taxpayer's Schedule OS income does not match AIS figures, the processing system flags a mismatch.
redFlags.consequence: Automated notice u/s 143(1)(a) for prima facie adjustment. Demand for additional tax on unreported income. Continued mismatches can trigger scrutiny u/s 143(2).
redFlags.correction: Download AIS and TIS from income tax portal before filing. Reconcile all dividend and interest entries. If AIS is incorrect (e.g., dividend reported twice), raise feedback on the portal against the specific entry before filing.

redFlags.title: Entering Aggregate LTCG Instead of Scrip-wise Details in Schedule 112A
redFlags.description: Schedule 112A requires scrip-by-scrip details including ISIN, number of units, dates of acquisition and sale, cost, FMV (where applicable), and sale price. Entering only aggregate LTCG amount without individual scrip details renders the schedule incomplete.
redFlags.consequence: Return processed with incomplete data. Income tax officer may disallow the exemption of ₹1.25 lakh and tax the entire LTCG. Also creates issues for grandfathering claims (FMV as on 31 Jan 2018).
redFlags.correction: Use broker-provided capital gains statement (most brokers provide ITR-ready CG statements). Enter each scrip/fund separately. For mutual funds, each scheme is treated as a separate scrip.

redFlags.title: Claiming Chapter VI-A Deductions After Opting for New Tax Regime
redFlags.description: Under the new tax regime, deductions u/s 80C, 80D, 80E, 80G, 80TTA, and most other Chapter VI-A deductions are NOT available. Only 80CCD(2) (employer NPS), 80CCH (Agniveer), and 80JJAA are available under the new regime.
redFlags.consequence: Inflated deductions under new regime. During return processing, system rejects these deductions and recomputes tax, potentially creating a demand.
redFlags.correction: If large deductions (>₹2.5 lakh) are available, compare old vs new regime tax before filing. If old regime is beneficial, explicitly opt for it using Form 10-IEA (for business income cases) or by selecting old regime in ITR before submission.

redFlags.title: Setting Off Long-Term Capital Loss Against Short-Term Capital Gains
redFlags.description: Long-Term Capital Loss (LTCL) can ONLY be set off against Long-Term Capital Gains (LTCG). It CANNOT be set off against Short-Term Capital Gains taxed u/s 111A or at slab rates. This restriction is statutory u/s 70(3).
redFlags.consequence: Inflated loss set-off reduces STCG incorrectly, leading to underpayment of tax. On scrutiny, the incorrectly set-off amount is added back and taxed with interest and penalty.
redFlags.correction: Apply STCL against both STCG and LTCG. Apply LTCL only against LTCG. Carry forward unadjusted LTCL for up to 8 assessment years.

redFlags.title: HUF Income Included in Karta's Individual ITR
redFlags.description: HUF is a separate taxable entity with its own PAN. HUF income must be reported in a separate ITR filed under the HUF's PAN. Clubbing HUF income in the Karta's personal ITR-2 is incorrect.
redFlags.consequence: HUF income taxed at wrong entity level, Karta's income inflated, potential double taxation. HUF return treated as not filed, attracting penalty u/s 271F (₹5,000).
redFlags.correction: Obtain HUF PAN if not already done. File a separate ITR-2 (or ITR-3 if HUF has business income) under HUF PAN. Report only Karta's personal income in individual ITR.

validationErrors.errorMessage: Director flag checked but no directorship details provided
validationErrors.resolution: Provide company name, CIN, and DIN under Director Disclosure section.

validationErrors.errorMessage: Schedule FA not filled despite income from foreign sources reported
validationErrors.resolution: Fill Schedule FA for all foreign assets. ROR individuals must complete this if foreign assets exist.

validationErrors.errorMessage: Schedule 112A missing for LTCG u/s 112A claimed in Schedule CG
validationErrors.resolution: Enter scrip-wise details in Schedule 112A for every equity share/MF unit sold at LTCG.

validationErrors.errorMessage: STCG u/s 111A rate used as 15% for post-23-July-2024 transactions
validationErrors.resolution: Apply 20% rate for transactions on or after 23 July 2024. Split transactions by date in Schedule CG.

validationErrors.errorMessage: Negative total income without carry-forward schedule
validationErrors.resolution: If total income is negative, fill Schedule CFL with details of losses to be carried forward.

validationErrors.errorMessage: HUF filing but 87A rebate claimed (only individual rebate applicable)
validationErrors.resolution: HUFs are not entitled to Section 87A rebate. Remove rebate claim from Part B-TTI.

validationErrors.errorMessage: NRI selected as residency status but Schedule FA filled
validationErrors.resolution: Schedule FA is required only for Resident and Ordinarily Resident (ROR) individuals. NRIs should not fill Schedule FA.

validationErrors.errorMessage: Deductions u/s 80C entered despite new regime selection
validationErrors.resolution: 80C deductions are not available under new regime. Either switch to old regime or remove 80C entries.

validationErrors.errorMessage: Self-assessment tax paid but BSR code or challan details missing
validationErrors.resolution: Enter BSR code, date of deposit, challan serial number, and amount paid for each challan.

validationErrors.errorMessage: Capital gains from equity MF included in OS instead of Schedule CG
validationErrors.resolution: Capital gains from equity MF redemption must be reported in Schedule CG/112A, not Schedule OS.
